text.title: Storybook Configuration Rules
text.summary: main.ts and preview.ts canonical rules for Storybook v8: framework selection, addon list, TypeScript docgen, and global decorator patterns.

rule.statement: Use @storybook/react-vite for Vite-based React projects. Use @storybook/nextjs for Next.js projects. Never mix frameworks: using react-vite with Next.js causes next/image and next/navigation to explode.
rule.rationale: Each framework adapter provides the correct module mocks for its routing and image system. Training data frequently produces react-vite configs for Next.js projects.

rule.statement: Always include: @storybook/addon-essentials, @storybook/addon-interactions, @storybook/addon-a11y. Add @chromatic-com/storybook for visual regression CI.
Pin Storybook version in package.json. Run npx storybook upgrade deliberately, not via Renovate auto-merge. Minor releases break configs.
Set reactDocgen: 'react-docgen-typescript' and filter out node_modules parent props. This generates Controls panel data from TypeScript interfaces automatically.

section.title: Global Decorators

rule.statement: One global decorator per cross-cutting concern (Theme, Router, QueryClient, i18n). Per-story decorators only for story-specific overrides. Never duplicate global decorators in individual story files.

section.reference: Storybook v8 main.ts with standard addons
Global preview with theme, ReactQuery, router decorators
